{{Quote|Teradoc had the gall to say to me that he'd only lend me toys if I would promise they would not return broken!|Ysanne Isard|<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Aggregator''''' was an [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] owned by [[High Admiral]] [[Treuten Teradoc]]. Prior to finding herself in Teradoc's service, it was used in campaigns against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the days of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. [[Ysanne Isard]] leased it in her quest to destroy [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].

==History==
Acquired by Isard from Admiral Teradoc, ''Aggregator'' was deployed along with Isard's [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Corrupter/Legends|''Corrupter'']] to the [[Alderaan/Legends|Graveyard]], lying in wait for [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] and the rest of the Rogues to capture them in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|system]]. The ''Aggregator'' exited hyperspace first to lay the trap, and the ''Corrupter'' followed shortly after to engage the Rogues and smugglers.

All went as planned, until the unexpected arrival of the [[Thranta-class War Cruiser|''Valiant'']]. The Rogues and ''Valiant'' critically damaged ''Corrupter'' to the degree that she was scuttled, later to be destroyed by the debris of Alderaan. ''Valiant'' afterwards engaged and brought down the ''Aggregator's'' shields, forcing the Interdictor's captain to shut off the gravity wells and divert power towards the shields. This move was too late, however, and several proton torpedoes penetrated the weakened defensive field, causing significant damage to the ship. ''Aggregator'' retreated into hyperspace, abandoning her [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] complement. Teradoc would never forgive Isard for almost losing his ship.

In the aftermath, [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Booster Terrik]] began circulating rumors that they were looking to sell a "used" Interdictor, making [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] like Teradoc even more reluctant to grant Isard access to their valuable ships.
